title: The Coffee Shop Regulars
text_zh: 我家楼下有一家不大的咖啡店，我几乎每天早上都去买一杯美式咖啡。时间长了以后，我发现这家店有一群固定的常客，每个人都有自己的习惯。最早到的是一个穿西装的中年男人，他每天七点准时到，点一杯黑咖啡，坐在靠窗的位置看报纸。然后是一个背着电脑包的年轻女生，她会点一杯拿铁，然后打开电脑工作到中午。角落里有一对退休的老夫妻，他们每周来三次，总是点两杯卡布奇诺和一份蛋糕两个人分着吃。我和他们从来没有说过话，但每次看到他们在自己固定的位置上，就觉得今天是正常的一天。有一次连续好几天没看到那个穿西装的男人，我居然有一点担心。后来他又出现了，一切照旧。咖啡店老板跟我说「你们这些常客比我还准时」。也许这就是城市生活的一种联系方式——不需要知道对方的名字，只需要知道他在。
text_pinyin: Wǒ jiā lóu xià yǒu yì jiā bú dà de kāfēi diàn, wǒ jīhū měi tiān zǎoshang dōu qù mǎi yì bēi měishì kāfēi. Shíjiān cháng le yǐhòu, wǒ fāxiàn zhè jiā diàn yǒu yì qún gùdìng de chángkè, měi gè rén dōu yǒu zìjǐ de xíguàn. Zuì zǎo dào de shì yí gè chuān xīzhuāng de zhōngnián nánrén, tā měi tiān qī diǎn zhǔnshí dào, diǎn yì bēi hēi kāfēi, zuò zài kào chuāng de wèizhì kàn bàozhǐ. Ránhòu shì yí gè bēi zhe diànnǎo bāo de niánqīng nǚshēng, tā huì diǎn yì bēi ná tiě, ránhòu dǎkāi diànnǎo gōngzuò dào zhōngwǔ. Jiǎoluò lǐ yǒu yí duì tuìxiū de lǎo fūqī, tāmen měi zhōu lái sān cì, zǒng shì diǎn liǎng bēi kǎbùqínuò hé yí fèn dàngāo liǎng gè rén fēn zhe chī. Wǒ hé tāmen cónglái méiyǒu shuō guò huà, dàn měi cì kàn dào tāmen zài zìjǐ gùdìng de wèizhì shàng, jiù juéde jīntiān shì zhèngcháng de yì tiān. Yǒu yí cì liánxù hǎo jǐ tiān méi kàn dào nàge chuān xīzhuāng de nánrén, wǒ jūrán yǒu yìdiǎn dānxīn. Hòulái tā yòu chūxiàn le, yíqiè zhào jiù. Kāfēi diàn lǎobǎn gēn wǒ shuō「nǐmen zhèxiē chángkè bǐ wǒ hái zhǔnshí」. Yěxǔ zhè jiù shì chéngshì shēnghuó de yì zhǒng liánxì fāngshì — bù xūyào zhīdào duìfāng de míngzi, zhǐ xūyào zhīdào tā zài.
text_en: There's a small coffee shop downstairs from my apartment. I buy an Americano there almost every morning. Over time, I've noticed the shop has a group of regulars, each with their own habits. The earliest is a middle-aged man in a suit who arrives at 7 sharp every day, orders a black coffee, and sits by the window reading the newspaper. Then a young woman with a laptop bag orders a latte and works on her computer until noon. In the corner sits a retired elderly couple who come three times a week, always ordering two cappuccinos and one cake to share. I've never spoken to any of them, but every time I see them in their usual spots, I feel today is a normal day. Once the suited man was absent for several days in a row, and I was actually a little worried. Then he reappeared, everything back to normal. The coffee shop owner told me 'you regulars are more punctual than I am.' Maybe this is one way city life connects people — you don't need to know someone's name, you just need to know they're there.

questions.q_zh: 那对退休老夫妻每次点什么？
questions.q_en: What does the retired couple always order?

options.text: 两杯美式和一份三明治
options.pinyin: liǎng bēi měishì hé yí fèn sānmíngzhì

options.text: 两杯卡布奇诺和一份蛋糕
options.pinyin: liǎng bēi kǎbùqínuò hé yí fèn dàngāo
yì bēi chá hé yí fèn miànbāo

questions.q_zh: 作者认为这种关系是什么？
questions.q_en: How does the author characterize this kind of relationship?

options.text: 不需要知道名字，只需要知道对方在
options.pinyin: bù xūyào zhīdào míngzi, zhǐ xūyào zhīdào duìfāng zài
options.text_en: No need for names, just knowing they're there

title: The Taxi Driver's Map
text_zh: 上周坐出租车的时候遇到了一个很健谈的司机。他开出租车已经十五年了，对这座城市的每一条路都非常熟悉。他说「以前没有导航的时候，全靠脑子记。这座城市的路我闭着眼睛都能开」。我问他这十五年城市变化大不大，他说「太大了。以前城东那一片全是农田，现在盖满了高楼。以前从南到北开车半个小时就够了，现在堵车的话要一两个小时」。他指着车窗外的一栋楼说「这里以前是一个很大的菜市场，我以前经常来这里买菜，老板娘做的豆腐特别好吃，后来拆了建了这个商场」。他说这些话的时候没有什么特别的感情，就像在说天气一样平淡。也许开了十五年的出租车，看多了变化，就学会了接受。他的脑子里有一张这座城市的地图，上面不只有路和建筑，还有那些已经消失的东西——一个菜市场、一条老街、一棵大树。地图上看不到的东西，他都记着。
text_pinyin: Shàng zhōu zuò chūzū chē de shíhou yù dào le yí gè hěn jiàntán de sījī. Tā kāi chūzū chē yǐjīng shíwǔ nián le, duì zhè zuò chéngshì de měi yì tiáo lù dōu fēicháng shúxī. Tā shuō「yǐqián méiyǒu dǎoháng de shíhou, quán kào nǎozi jì. Zhè zuò chéngshì de lù wǒ bì zhe yǎnjīng dōu néng kāi」. Wǒ wèn tā zhè shíwǔ nián chéngshì biànhuà dà bú dà, tā shuō「tài dà le. Yǐqián chéng dōng nà yí piàn quán shì nóngtián, xiànzài gài mǎn le gāolóu. Yǐqián cóng nán dào běi kāi chē bàn gè xiǎoshí jiù gòu le, xiànzài dǔ chē de huà yào yì liǎng gè xiǎoshí」. Tā zhǐ zhe chē chuāng wài de yí dòng lóu shuō「zhèlǐ yǐqián shì yí gè hěn dà de cài shìchǎng, wǒ yǐqián jīngcháng lái zhèlǐ mǎi cài, lǎobǎn niáng zuò de dòufu tèbié hǎochī, hòulái chāi le jiàn le zhège shāngchǎng」. Tā shuō zhèxiē huà de shíhou méiyǒu shénme tèbié de gǎnqíng, jiù xiàng zài shuō tiānqì yíyàng píngdàn. Yěxǔ kāi le shíwǔ nián de chūzū chē, kàn duō le biànhuà, jiù xuéhuì le jiēshòu. Tā de nǎozi lǐ yǒu yì zhāng zhè zuò chéngshì de dìtú, shàngmiàn bù zhǐ yǒu lù hé jiànzhù, hái yǒu nàxiē yǐjīng xiāoshī de dōngxi — yí gè cài shìchǎng, yì tiáo lǎo jiē, yì kē dà shù. Dìtú shàng kàn bú dào de dōngxi, tā dōu jì zhe.
text_en: Last week I met a very talkative taxi driver. He'd been driving a cab for fifteen years and knew every road in the city. He said 'Before navigation, it was all memory. I could drive this city's roads with my eyes closed.' I asked if the city had changed much in fifteen years. He said 'Tremendously. The east side was all farmland before, now covered in towers. Driving north to south used to take half an hour, now it takes an hour or two with traffic.' He pointed at a building outside the window: 'This used to be a big vegetable market. I used to buy groceries here, the owner's wife made amazing tofu. They demolished it and built this mall.' He said all this without particular emotion, as casually as discussing the weather. Maybe after fifteen years of driving a cab and seeing so much change, you learn to accept. In his mind is a map of this city — showing not just roads and buildings, but things that have disappeared: a vegetable market, an old street, a big tree. The things you can't see on a map, he remembers them all.

questions.q_zh: 以前城东那一片是什么？
questions.q_en: What used to be on the east side of the city?

questions.q_zh: 司机脑子里的地图有什么特别的？
questions.q_en: What's special about the driver's mental map?

options.pinyin: jì zhe yǐjīng xiāoshī de dōngxi

title: The Park Bench
text_zh: 公园里有一张绿色的长椅，就在湖边的一棵大柳树下面。我每个周末都会来这里坐一会儿。这张椅子不知道为什么特别舒服，靠背的角度刚刚好，坐上去以后整个人会自然地放松下来。柳树的枝条垂下来，风一吹就轻轻地摇晃，有时候会扫到你的肩膀。湖面上有几只鸭子游来游去，偶尔把头伸进水里找吃的。对面有几个老人在打太极拳，动作很慢很优美，像在水中游泳一样。坐久了以后你会注意到各种各样的人经过：牵着小狗散步的年轻人、推着婴儿车的妈妈、手拉着手的情侣、独自跑步的人。他们来了又走了，但椅子一直在这里。我想过很多人可能都在这张椅子上坐过，每个人来的时候带着不同的心情，走的时候也许会好一些。一张公园的椅子不会说话，但它默默地接纳了每一个需要坐下来休息一会儿的人。
text_pinyin: Gōngyuán lǐ yǒu yì zhāng lǜsè de cháng yǐ, jiù zài hú biān de yì kē dà liǔ shù xiàmiàn. Wǒ měi gè zhōumò dōu huì lái zhèlǐ zuò yíhuìr. Zhè zhāng yǐzi bù zhīdào wèishénme tèbié shūfu, kào bèi de jiǎodù gānggāng hǎo, zuò shàngqù yǐhòu zhěnggè rén huì zìrán de fàngsōng xiàlái. Liǔ shù de zhītiáo chuí xiàlái, fēng yí chuī jiù qīngqīng de yáohuàng, yǒu shíhou huì sǎo dào nǐ de jiānbǎng. Hú miàn shàng yǒu jǐ zhī yāzi yóu lái yóu qù, ǒu'ěr bǎ tóu shēn jìn shuǐ lǐ zhǎo chī de. Duìmiàn yǒu jǐ gè lǎorén zài dǎ tàijí quán, dòngzuò hěn màn hěn yōuměi, xiàng zài shuǐ zhōng yóuyǒng yíyàng. Zuò jiǔ le yǐhòu nǐ huì zhùyì dào gè zhǒng gè yàng de rén jīngguò: qiān zhe xiǎo gǒu sànbù de niánqīng rén, tuī zhe yīng'ér chē de māma, shǒu lā zhe shǒu de qínglǚ, dúzì pǎobù de rén. Tāmen lái le yòu zǒu le, dàn yǐzi yìzhí zài zhèlǐ. Wǒ xiǎng guò hěn duō rén kěnéng dōu zài zhè zhāng yǐzi shàng zuò guò, měi gè rén lái de shíhou dài zhe bùtóng de xīnqíng, zǒu de shíhou yěxǔ huì hǎo yìxiē. Yì zhāng gōngyuán de yǐzi bú huì shuōhuà, dàn tā mòmò de jiēnà le měi yí gè xūyào zuò xiàlái xiūxi yíhuìr de rén.
text_en: In the park there's a green bench, right under a big willow tree by the lake. Every weekend I come sit here for a while. The bench is somehow especially comfortable — the backrest angle is just right, and sitting down you naturally relax. Willow branches hang down, swaying gently in the breeze, sometimes brushing your shoulders. A few ducks swim around on the lake, occasionally dipping heads underwater to find food. Across the way some elders practice tai chi, movements slow and graceful, like swimming through water. After sitting long enough you notice all sorts of people passing: young people walking small dogs, mothers pushing strollers, couples holding hands, solo joggers. They come and go, but the bench is always here. I've thought about how many people have probably sat on this bench, each arriving with a different mood, perhaps leaving feeling a little better. A park bench can't speak, but it silently accepts everyone who needs to sit down and rest a moment.

questions.q_zh: 长椅在公园的什么位置？
questions.q_en: Where is the bench in the park?

options.pinyin: hú biān yì kē dà liǔ shù xià
options.text_en: Under a big willow tree by the lake
In the middle of the garden

questions.q_zh: 作者觉得公园的椅子做了什么？
questions.q_en: What does the author think the park bench does?

options.pinyin: zhǐ shì yí gè pǔtōng de jiājù
options.text_en: Just an ordinary piece of furniture

options.text: 默默地接纳了每个需要休息的人
options.pinyin: mòmò de jiēnà le měi gè xūyào xiūxi de rén
options.text_en: Silently accepts everyone who needs to rest

options.pinyin: yīnggāi huàn chéng gèng hǎo de yǐzi
options.text_en: Should be replaced with a better chair

title: The Vegetable Delivery
text_zh: 每天凌晨四点左右，一辆绿色的小货车会停在我们小区旁边的超市门口。司机是一个皮肤黝黑的中年男人，他从后面打开车门，车里装满了一筐一筐的蔬菜和水果。超市还没有开门，但已经有一个店员等在那里了。两个人配合着把菜一筐一筐地搬进去，动作很快，几乎不说话。整个过程大概二十分钟。有一次我因为失眠起得很早，看到了这个场景。那些摆在超市里整整齐齐的蔬菜，原来是在大部分人还在睡觉的时候就送到了。我想到了这些菜的旅程：前一天下午在某个农场被采摘，然后送到批发市场分装，深夜装上货车，凌晨到达超市。每一步都有人在工作。等到我上午九十点去买菜的时候，这些菜已经洗干净了，贴上了价格标签，安安静静地躺在货架上。它们看起来好像一直就在那里，但其实它们经历了很长的旅程才到这里。
text_pinyin: Měi tiān língchén sì diǎn zuǒyòu, yí liàng lǜsè de xiǎo huòchē huì tíng zài wǒmen xiǎoqū pángbiān de chāoshì ménkǒu. Sījī shì yí gè pífū yǒu hēi de zhōngnián nánrén, tā cóng hòumiàn dǎkāi chē mén, chē lǐ zhuāng mǎn le yì kuāng yì kuāng de shūcài hé shuǐguǒ. Chāoshì hái méiyǒu kāi mén, dàn yǐjīng yǒu yí gè diànyuán děng zài nàlǐ le. Liǎng gè rén pèihé zhe bǎ cài yì kuāng yì kuāng de bān jìnqù, dòngzuò hěn kuài, jīhū bù shuōhuà. Zhěnggè guòchéng dàgài èrshí fēnzhōng. Yǒu yí cì wǒ yīnwèi shīmián qǐ de hěn zǎo, kàn dào le zhège chǎngjǐng. Nàxiē bǎi zài chāoshì lǐ zhěngzhěng qíqí de shūcài, yuánlái shì zài dà bùfen rén hái zài shuìjiào de shíhou jiù sòng dào le. Wǒ xiǎng dào le zhèxiē cài de lǚchéng: qián yì tiān xiàwǔ zài mǒu gè nóngchǎng bèi cǎizhāi, ránhòu sòng dào pīfā shìchǎng fēnzhuāng, shēnyè zhuāng shàng huòchē, língchén dàodá chāoshì. Měi yí bù dōu yǒu rén zài gōngzuò. Děng dào wǒ shàngwǔ jiǔ shí diǎn qù mǎi cài de shíhou, zhèxiē cài yǐjīng xǐ gānjìng le, tiē shàng le jiàgé biāoqiān, ān ān jìng jìng de tǎng zài huòjià shàng. Tāmen kàn qǐlái hǎoxiàng yìzhí jiù zài nàlǐ, dàn qíshí tāmen jīnglì le hěn cháng de lǚchéng cái dào zhèlǐ.
text_en: Every day around 4 AM, a green delivery truck parks outside the supermarket next to our compound. The driver is a dark-skinned middle-aged man. He opens the rear doors, the truck packed with crates of vegetables and fruit. The supermarket hasn't opened yet, but an employee is already waiting. The two work together, carrying crates inside one by one, moving quickly, barely speaking. The whole process takes about twenty minutes. Once I woke early from insomnia and witnessed this scene. Those vegetables arranged neatly in the supermarket — they arrive while most people are still sleeping. I thought about these vegetables' journey: picked the previous afternoon at some farm, sent to a wholesale market for sorting, loaded onto a truck late at night, arriving at the supermarket at dawn. At every step, someone is working. By the time I go shopping at 9 or 10 AM, these vegetables have been washed, price-tagged, lying quietly on shelves. They look as if they've always been there, but they actually traveled a long journey to get here.

questions.q_en: Around what time does the delivery truck arrive?

questions.q_zh: 作者为什么能看到送菜的场景？
questions.q_en: Why was the author able to see the delivery scene?

options.pinyin: yīnwèi shīmián qǐ de hěn zǎo

title: The Laundromat
text_zh: 我家附近新开了一家自助洗衣店，二十四小时营业。里面有十台洗衣机和六台烘干机，全部是投币或者扫码使用。没有店员，也没有收银台，只有一台自助缴费的机器和一个留言板。留言板上贴着一些小纸条，有人写着「请不要占用烘干机太久」，有人写着「谁的蓝色袜子忘在三号机器里了」，还有人画了一个笑脸写着「祝大家衣服都洗干净」。我喜欢在等衣服洗好的那四十分钟里坐在里面的塑料椅子上看书。洗衣机运转的声音有一种催眠的效果，嗡嗡嗡的，很规律。偶尔有人进来放衣服或者取衣服，大家互相点个头就行了。有一次一个年轻人进来，发现自己没有零钱也没有手机信号，我帮他扫码付了钱。他说「谢谢」，我说「没事」。这就是自助洗衣店里人与人之间交流的全部了。简单，但够了。
text_pinyin: Wǒ jiā fùjìn xīn kāi le yì jiā zìzhù xǐyī diàn, èrshísì xiǎoshí yíngyè. Lǐmiàn yǒu shí tái xǐyījī hé liù tái hōnggānjī, quánbù shì tóu bì huòzhě sǎo mǎ shǐyòng. Méiyǒu diànyuán, yě méiyǒu shōuyín tái, zhǐ yǒu yì tái zìzhù jiǎofèi de jīqì hé yí gè liúyán bǎn. Liúyán bǎn shàng tiē zhe yìxiē xiǎo zhǐtiáo, yǒu rén xiě zhe「qǐng bú yào zhànyòng hōnggānjī tài jiǔ」, yǒu rén xiě zhe「shéi de lán sè wàzi wàng zài sān hào jīqì lǐ le」, hái yǒu rén huà le yí gè xiàoliǎn xiě zhe「zhù dàjiā yīfu dōu xǐ gānjìng」. Wǒ xǐhuan zài děng yīfu xǐ hǎo de nà sìshí fēnzhōng lǐ zuò zài lǐmiàn de sùliào yǐzi shàng kàn shū. Xǐyījī yùnzhuǎn de shēngyīn yǒu yì zhǒng cuīmián de xiàoguǒ, wēng wēng wēng de, hěn guīlǜ. Ǒu'ěr yǒu rén jìnlái fàng yīfu huòzhě qǔ yīfu, dàjiā hùxiāng diǎn gè tóu jiù xíng le. Yǒu yí cì yí gè niánqīng rén jìnlái, fāxiàn zìjǐ méiyǒu língqián yě méiyǒu shǒujī xìnhào, wǒ bāng tā sǎo mǎ fù le qián. Tā shuō「xièxie」, wǒ shuō「méi shì」. Zhè jiù shì zìzhù xǐyī diàn lǐ rén yǔ rén zhījiān jiāoliú de quánbù le. Jiǎndān, dàn gòu le.
text_en: A new self-service laundromat opened near my home, operating 24 hours. Inside are ten washing machines and six dryers, all coin or QR code operated. No staff, no register — just a self-service payment machine and a bulletin board. The board has little notes: someone wrote 'please don't hog the dryer too long,' someone wrote 'whose blue socks were left in machine #3,' and someone drew a smiley face with 'wishing everyone clean clothes.' I like sitting on the plastic chairs inside reading during the forty minutes my clothes wash. The washing machine's hum has a hypnotic effect — steady and rhythmic. Occasionally someone comes in to load or retrieve clothes; everyone just nods to each other. Once a young man came in and found he had neither coins nor phone signal; I scanned the code and paid for him. He said 'thanks,' I said 'no problem.' That's the entirety of human interaction in a self-service laundromat. Simple, but enough.

options.pinyin: kèrén xiě de gè zhǒng xiǎo zhǐtiáo
options.text_en: Various little notes written by customers

questions.q_zh: 作者在等衣服洗好的时候做什么？
questions.q_en: What does the author do while waiting for clothes to wash?

options.pinyin: zuò zài sùliào yǐzi shàng kàn shū
options.text_en: Sits on a plastic chair reading

title: The Street After Rain
text_zh: 下了一整个下午的雨终于停了。我推开窗户，空气里有一股清新的味道，像是大地刚刚洗了一个澡。楼下的街道湿漉漉的，路面反射着路灯的光，像一面镜子。树叶上挂着水珠，风一吹就落下来几颗，掉在地上发出很轻很轻的声音。我决定出去走走。雨后的城市特别安静，行人比平时少很多。路边的积水映着对面商店招牌的灯光，红色和黄色的光在水面上晃动，好像城市在水里有了另一个自己。一辆车开过去，轮胎压过水面发出「刷」的一声，水花溅起来又落下去。我走到一个小广场，发现有几个小孩在积水里跳来跳去，鞋子已经全湿了，但他们笑得很开心。他们的妈妈站在旁边皱着眉头但也忍不住笑了。也许雨后的城市最好的礼物就是这些积水——给大人一面镜子照一照被洗干净的世界，给小孩一个跳来跳去的游乐场。
text_pinyin: Xià le yí zhěng gè xiàwǔ de yǔ zhōngyú tíng le. Wǒ tuī kāi chuānghu, kōngqì lǐ yǒu yì gǔ qīngxīn de wèidào, xiàng shì dàdì gānggāng xǐ le yí gè zǎo. Lóu xià de jiēdào shī lù lù de, lù miàn fǎnshè zhe lùdēng de guāng, xiàng yí miàn jìngzi. Shùyè shàng guà zhe shuǐzhū, fēng yí chuī jiù luò xiàlái jǐ kē, diào zài dì shàng fāchū hěn qīng hěn qīng de shēngyīn. Wǒ juédìng chūqù zǒu zǒu. Yǔ hòu de chéngshì tèbié ānjìng, xíngrén bǐ píngshí shǎo hěn duō. Lù biān de jī shuǐ yìng zhe duìmiàn shāngdiàn zhāopai de dēngguāng, hóngsè hé huángsè de guāng zài shuǐ miàn shàng huàngdòng, hǎoxiàng chéngshì zài shuǐ lǐ yǒu le lìng yí gè zìjǐ. Yí liàng chē kāi guòqù, lúntāi yā guò shuǐ miàn fāchū「shuā」de yì shēng, shuǐhuā jiàn qǐlái yòu luò xiàqù. Wǒ zǒu dào yí gè xiǎo guǎngchǎng, fāxiàn yǒu jǐ gè xiǎohái zài jī shuǐ lǐ tiào lái tiào qù, xiézi yǐjīng quán shī le, dàn tāmen xiào de hěn kāixīn. Tāmen de māma zhàn zài pángbiān zhòu zhe méitóu dàn yě rěn bú zhù xiào le. Yěxǔ yǔ hòu de chéngshì zuì hǎo de lǐwù jiù shì zhèxiē jī shuǐ — gěi dàrén yí miàn jìngzi zhào yí zhào bèi xǐ gānjìng de shìjiè, gěi xiǎohái yí gè tiào lái tiào qù de yóulèchǎng.
text_en: The rain that fell all afternoon finally stopped. I pushed open the window — the air had a fresh smell, as if the earth had just taken a bath. The street below was wet, the road surface reflecting streetlights like a mirror. Water droplets hung on leaves, a few falling when the wind blew, hitting the ground with the faintest sound. I decided to go for a walk. The post-rain city was especially quiet, far fewer pedestrians than usual. Puddles by the road reflected shop sign lights opposite — red and yellow light rippling on the water, as if the city had another self inside the puddles. A car drove past, tires swishing through water, splashing up and falling back down. I walked to a small plaza and found several children jumping in puddles, shoes completely soaked but laughing happily. Their mother stood nearby frowning but couldn't help laughing too. Maybe the post-rain city's best gift is these puddles — giving adults a mirror to see a freshly washed world, giving children a playground to jump in.

questions.q_zh: 雨后的路面看起来像什么？
questions.q_en: What does the road surface look like after rain?
What are the children doing in the puddles?

title: The Old Neighborhood
text_zh: 我长大的那个老小区现在还在，但已经变了很多。上周我回去看了一下。小区门口的保安换了人，以前是一个很和蔼的老大爷，总是笑着和每个人打招呼，现在换成了一个戴墨镜的年轻人，不太爱说话。进门以后发现路面重新铺过了，以前坑坑洼洼的水泥路变成了平整的柏油路。小花园还在，但里面的大树被砍了几棵，说是根太大影响了地下管道。我家住过的那栋楼外墙重新刷了漆，原来的淡黄色变成了灰白色。窗户还是老式的铝合金窗户，但有些住户已经换成了新的。楼下那个我小时候经常玩的滑梯还在，但明显旧了很多，颜色也褪了。我站在那里看了一会儿，觉得这个地方既熟悉又陌生。那些记忆中的画面像是贴在玻璃上的旧照片，透过它们可以看到后面新的风景，但照片本身已经开始变黄了。
text_pinyin: Wǒ zhǎng dà de nàge lǎo xiǎoqū xiànzài hái zài, dàn yǐjīng biàn le hěn duō. Shàng zhōu wǒ huíqù kàn le yíxià. Xiǎoqū ménkǒu de bǎo'ān huàn le rén, yǐqián shì yí gè hěn hé'ǎi de lǎo dàyé, zǒng shì xiào zhe hé měi gè rén dǎ zhāohu, xiànzài huàn chéng le yí gè dài mòjìng de niánqīng rén, bú tài ài shuōhuà. Jìn mén yǐhòu fāxiàn lùmiàn chóngxīn pū guò le, yǐqián kēngkēngwāwā de shuǐní lù biàn chéng le píngzhěng de bǎiyóu lù. Xiǎo huāyuán hái zài, dàn lǐmiàn de dà shù bèi kǎn le jǐ kē, shuō shì gēn tài dà yǐngxiǎng le dìxià guǎndào. Wǒ jiā zhù guò de nà dòng lóu wàiqiáng chóngxīn shuā le qī, yuánlái de dàn huángsè biàn chéng le huī báisè. Chuānghu háishi lǎo shì de lǚ héjīn chuānghu, dàn yǒuxiē zhùhù yǐjīng huàn chéng le xīn de. Lóu xià nàge wǒ xiǎo shíhou jīngcháng wán de huátī hái zài, dàn míngxiǎn jiù le hěn duō, yánsè yě tuì le. Wǒ zhàn zài nàlǐ kàn le yíhuìr, juéde zhège dìfāng jì shúxī yòu mòshēng. Nàxiē jìyì zhōng de huàmiàn xiàng shì tiē zài bōli shàng de jiù zhàopiàn, tòuguò tāmen kěyǐ kàn dào hòumiàn xīn de fēngjǐng, dàn zhàopiàn běnshēn yǐjīng kāishǐ biàn huáng le.
text_en: The old compound where I grew up is still there, but has changed a lot. I went back last week to look. The gate security has been replaced — before it was a kindly old man who always greeted everyone with a smile, now it's a young man in sunglasses who doesn't talk much. Inside, the roads have been repaved — the bumpy concrete replaced with smooth asphalt. The small garden is still there, but some big trees were cut down, apparently their roots interfered with underground pipes. The building I lived in has been repainted — from pale yellow to gray-white. The windows are still old-style aluminum frames, though some residents have replaced theirs with new ones. The slide downstairs that I played on as a child is still there, but obviously much older, colors faded. I stood there for a while, feeling the place was both familiar and strange. Those images from memory are like old photos stuck on glass — you can see the new scenery behind them, but the photos themselves have started to yellow.

questions.q_zh: 小区的路变成了什么样？
questions.q_en: How have the compound's roads changed?

options.text: 从坑洼的水泥路变成了平整的柏油路
options.pinyin: cóng kēngwā de shuǐní lù biàn chéng le píngzhěng de bǎiyóu lù
options.text_en: From bumpy concrete to smooth asphalt

questions.q_zh: 作者回去以后的感觉是什么？
questions.q_en: How did the author feel upon returning?

title: The Bus Stop Shelter
text_zh: 有一天突然下起了大雨，我没有带伞，跑到最近的公交站的遮雨棚下面躲雨。棚子不大，已经挤了七八个人了。大家站得很近，但都很安静，只有雨打在棚顶的声音。一个穿校服的女孩把书包抱在怀里怕淋湿，一个穿工作服的男人在看手机上的天气预报，一个提着菜篮子的老太太嘀咕着「这雨什么时候停」。我们都是陌生人，因为同一场雨被困在了同一个小小的空间里。这种感觉很奇特——平时在城市里大家各走各的路，谁也不看谁。但是一场雨就把我们聚在了一起，共享着同一个屋檐。过了大概十分钟，雨小了一些。有人先走了，有人还在等。一辆公交车来了，上去了两三个人。又过了一会儿，雨停了，大家陆续离开，各自走向不同的方向。棚子又空了，等着下一场雨、下一群陌生人。
text_pinyin: Yǒu yì tiān tūrán xià qǐ le dà yǔ, wǒ méiyǒu dài sǎn, pǎo dào zuì jìn de gōngjiāo zhàn de zhē yǔ péng xiàmiàn duǒ yǔ. Péngzi bú dà, yǐjīng jǐ le qī bā gè rén le. Dàjiā zhàn de hěn jìn, dàn dōu hěn ānjìng, zhǐ yǒu yǔ dǎ zài péng dǐng de shēngyīn. Yí gè chuān xiàofú de nǚhái bǎ shūbāo bào zài huái lǐ pà lín shī, yí gè chuān gōngzuò fú de nánrén zài kàn shǒujī shàng de tiānqì yùbào, yí gè tí zhe cài lánzi de lǎo tàitai dīgu zhe「zhè yǔ shénme shíhou tíng」. Wǒmen dōu shì mòshēng rén, yīnwèi tóng yì chǎng yǔ bèi kùn zài le tóng yí gè xiǎo xiǎo de kōngjiān lǐ. Zhè zhǒng gǎnjué hěn qítè — píngshí zài chéngshì lǐ dàjiā gè zǒu gè de lù, shéi yě bú kàn shéi. Dànshì yì chǎng yǔ jiù bǎ wǒmen jù zài le yìqǐ, gòngxiǎng zhe tóng yí gè wūyán. Guò le dàgài shí fēnzhōng, yǔ xiǎo le yìxiē. Yǒu rén xiān zǒu le, yǒu rén hái zài děng. Yí liàng gōngjiāo chē lái le, shàngqù le liǎng sān gè rén. Yòu guò le yíhuìr, yǔ tíng le, dàjiā lùxù líkāi, gèzì zǒu xiàng bùtóng de fāngxiàng. Péngzi yòu kōng le, děng zhe xià yì chǎng yǔ, xià yì qún mòshēng rén.
text_en: One day a sudden downpour started and I had no umbrella. I ran to the nearest bus stop shelter to hide from the rain. The shelter was small, already packed with seven or eight people. Everyone stood close together but stayed quiet, only the sound of rain hitting the roof. A girl in school uniform clutched her backpack to her chest to keep it dry. A man in work clothes checked the weather forecast on his phone. An old lady with a grocery basket muttered 'when is this rain going to stop.' We were all strangers, trapped in the same small space by the same rain. The feeling was peculiar — normally in the city everyone walks their own path, nobody looking at each other. But one rainstorm gathered us together, sharing the same eave. After about ten minutes the rain eased. Some left first, others kept waiting. A bus came, two or three people got on. A while later the rain stopped, everyone gradually departed in different directions. The shelter was empty again, waiting for the next rain, the next group of strangers.

questions.q_zh: 遮雨棚下面挤了多少人？
questions.q_en: How many people were crowded under the shelter?

options.pinyin: zhǐ yǒu zuòzhě yí gè rén

questions.q_zh: 作者觉得这场雨做了什么？
questions.q_en: What does the author think the rain did?

options.text: 把陌生人聚在了一起共享同一个屋檐
options.pinyin: bǎ mòshēng rén jù zài le yìqǐ gòngxiǎng tóng yí gè wūyán
options.text_en: Gathered strangers together under the same eave

options.pinyin: ràng chéngshì biàn de gèng rènao
zhǐ shì pǔtōng de zìrán xiànxiàng
